input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image showing seven CAD views (each boxed in black) of our vehicle design. The top, big view shows a close-up view of the design. The six smaller views on the bottom of the image show different complete views of the CAD of the vehicle and are provided for context. Note that the close-up view orientation matches one of the six complete view orientations. The close-up view may also have some components hidden (with respect to the corresponding complete view) for visualization of specific components. Looking at the close-up view, is/are any part of the the main hoop visible in the close-up view? Answer simply with yes or no.

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.5.9.3
            TITLE: in the side view of the frame, the main hoop and the main hoop braces must not lie on the

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.9.3: In the side view of the Frame, the Main Hoop and the Main Hoop Braces must not lie on the
F.5.9.3 In the side view of the Frame, the Main Hoop and the Main Hoop Braces must not lie on the 
same side of the vertical line through the top of the Main Hoop.   
(If the Main Hoop leans forward, the Braces must be forward of the Main Hoop, and if the 
Main Hoop leans rearward, the Braces must be rearward of the Main Hoop)

RULE: f.1.4
            TITLE: main hoop

            CONTENT:
            rule F.1.4: Main Hoop
F.1.4 Main Hoop 
A roll bar located alongside or immediately aft of the driver’s torso.

RULE: f.5.9.7
            TITLE: the main hoop braces must be:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.9.7: The Main Hoop Braces must be:
F.5.9.7 The Main Hoop Braces must be:  
a. Securely integrated into the Frame 
b. Capable of transmitting all loads from the Main Hoop into the Major Structure of the 
Chassis without failing

RULE: f.5.9.2
            TITLE: the main hoop must be supported by two braces extending in the forward or rearward

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.9.2: The Main Hoop must be supported by two Braces extending in the forward or rearward
F.5.9.2 The Main Hoop must be supported by two Braces extending in the forward or rearward 
direction, one on each of the left and right sides of the Main Hoop.

RULE: f.5.9.5
            TITLE: the included angle formed by the main hoop and the main hoop braces must be 30° or more.

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.9.5: The included angle formed by the Main Hoop and the Main Hoop Braces must be 30° or more.
F.5.9.5 The included angle formed by the Main Hoop and the Main Hoop Braces must be 30° or more.
